title: 10 best herbal teas for dry eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein teas helps with dry eyes because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. The saponins in mullein have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of dry eye disease by moisturizing the eyes and promoting the production of tears. Additionally, mullein's emollient properties help to lubricate and protect the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from the discomfort and itching associated with dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in mullein tea, including vitamin E and beta-carotene, protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry eye conditions.
Soothing and calming effects: Mullein tea's flavonoids and mucilage (a thick, protective substance) help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing discomfort and irritation associated with dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and lubricate dry eyes.
Leaves: Contain antioxidants that help protect and moisturize the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried mullein leaves from verbascum thapsus plants.
Measure one teaspoon of dried mullein leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves per cup water.
Heat one cup of water to boiling and add the measured mullein leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with dry eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tea's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, reduce inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of dry eye syndrome. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye dryness. As a natural remedy, Kansas coneflower tea provides long-lasting relief for individuals suffering from dry, itchy, or gritty eyes, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate dryness.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in the tea can neutralize free radicals that can damage the cornea and conjunctiva, reducing oxidative stress and promoting eye health.

Soften and moisten mucous membranes: Kansas Coneflower contains mucilages, which are natural substances that can help soften and moisturize the mucous membranes of the eyes, providing relief from dryness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, seeds promote healthy eye tissue and reduce inflammation associated with dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to release.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the tea to enhance its sweetness and potential therapeutic effects.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it, ideally within 30-40 minutes after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with dry eyes because its active compound, flavonoids, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and irritation. The tea's antioxidant-rich profile also soothes and protects the delicate tissues of the eyes, combating oxidative stress that can exacerbate dryness. Additionally, pot marigold contains mucilages, which form a protective film on the surface of the eyes, providing long-lasting hydration and relief from discomfort. This natural remedy effectively alleviates the symptoms of dry eyes, promoting healthy vision and comfortable eye function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marigold tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in marigold tea, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to dry eye symptoms like blurred vision and eye fatigue.

Sotheing and moisturizing effects: Marigold tea contains mucilages, which are thick, protective compounds that can soothe and lubricate the eyes, helping to alleviate dryness, itchiness, and irritation associated with dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids that protect the eyes from damage and promote healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried calendula flowers and 1 quart of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a large ceramic teapot.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea and stir well before serving.
Drink the tea while warm, ideally 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with dry eyes because of its unique properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The apigenin, a flavonoid found in chamomile, binds to alpha receptors in the eye, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dryness. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce redness and swelling, while its antioxidant properties protect the eyes from further damage. Drinking chamomile tea can provide immediate relief, providing a gentle and natural remedy for dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from dryness and itchiness.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in chamomile tea, including flavonoids and terpenoids, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome.
Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's gentle, sedative properties help relax the eyelids and surrounding tissues, reducing redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itchiness associated with dry eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes and calms irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the chamomile mixture for five to seven minutes in order to allow optimal flavonoid extraction.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solid particles.
Add one tablespoon of honey or other sweetener as desired to enhance flavor and palatability.
Allow the tea to cool for three to five minutes before drinking to minimize eye irritation potential.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop teas helps with dry eyes because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties. When steeped in hot water, these compounds are released into the tea, creating a soothing brew that can help calm irritated eyes. The hydrating properties of the tea also work to moisturize and refresh the eyes, reducing redness and itchiness. Additionally, the antihistamine properties of hyssop may help alleviate itchy or watery eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hyssop tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms such as irritation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in hyssop tea, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation in the conjunctiva, a thin membrane covering the white part of the eye, which can alleviate dryness and itchiness.

Antimicrobial properties: Hyssop tea's antimicrobial compounds may help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to chronic dry eye syndrome by reducing the risk of secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and protecting dry eyes.
Leaves: Hyssop leaves contain flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and improve vision, making them a popular choice for treating dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried hyssop leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured hyssop leaves with the boiling water in a large glass teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on personal preference and desired strength of tea.
Strain the liquid from the solids using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before refrigerating it in an airtight container for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with dry eyes because it contains glycyrretinic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When brewed into a tea, this compound is absorbed into the body and helps to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to dryness and discomfort. Additionally, the soothing properties of licorice root may help to calm irritated eyes and provide relief from itchy, gritty sensations associated with dry eye syndrome. Regular consumption of herbal licorice teas may offer a natural and effective way to alleviate dry eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes caused by dryness.
Licoric acid: This flavonoid has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections that can exacerbate dry eye syndrome.

Furostanol saponins: These compounds have been found to have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, including those in the eyes, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with dry eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, licorice root extract helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Containing glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation, licorice bark tea can help alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Fruits: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, licorice fruits contribute to the antioxidant properties of the tea, protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried licorice root from glycyrrhiza glabra plants, ideally in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the licorice root further if it's fresh by spreading it thinly on paper towels for several days to prevent mold growth.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of dried licorice root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, taking care not to inhale dust particles.
Combine the powdered licorice root with boiling water in a teapot or mug, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds like glycyrrhizin.
Strain the tea into another container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving hot or warm to soothe dry eyes within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark teas helps with dry eyes because it contains a unique compound called catechins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve ocular hydration. remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried cinchona pubescens bark and rinse it thoroughly under cold running water.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in cleavers tea help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome and other eye disorders.
Moisturizing properties: Cleavers tea contains compounds that can help to hydrate and soothe the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort associated with dry eye disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eye.
Rhyzomes: Contains mucilages that soothe and lubricate the eyes, providing relief from dry, itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems, ensuring proper identification and cleanliness.
Rinse the harvested plant material in cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried galium aparine herb with 1 cup boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the herbal mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain and discard the solids, then serve the warm tea immediately and drink as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed teas helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The herb's natural antioxidants also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and burning sensations. Additionally, chickweed's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, locking in moisture and reducing dryness. As a result, chickweed teas provide relief from dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chickweed tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in chickweed tea can help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and exacerbate dry eye conditions, promoting overall eye health and reducing oxidative stress.

Soothing demulcent properties: Chickweed's demulcent properties create a protective film on the eyes and surrounding tissue, providing relief from irritation, redness, and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chickweed roots are a popular choice for making teas due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes.

Seeds: Chickweed seeds contain omega-6 fatty acids and antioxidants that work together to reduce inflammation and promote healthy eye function, making them an effective addition to medicinal teas for dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried stellaria media flowers for every cup of boiling water you plan to use.
Pour the boiling water over the dried flowers in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Drink the tea immediately after straining while it is still warm or let it cool down to room temperature if preferred.
Consume one to two cups of stellaria media tea per day as needed for relief from dry eyes symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry eyes with herbal teas is to brew soothing herbs like chamomile, calendula, or green tea. These teas help to reduce inflammation and moisturize the eyes. Drink 1-2 cups daily, warm or at room temperature, and use a clean cloth or cotton ball to apply cooled tea bags over closed eyes for 5-10 minutes. This gentle treatment helps to calm dry, irritated eyes and promote overall eye health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry eyes that are treated with herbal teas are irritations such as conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and meibomian gland dysfunction. Herbal teas like calendula, chamomile, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing redness, itching, and discomfort. These teas can be used as a warm compress or applied directly to the eyes to provide relief from these conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry eyes are natural remedies that help alleviate dry eye symptoms. These teas work by reducing inflammation and increasing tear production, providing much-needed relief for those who suffer from dry, itchy eyes. Examples include peppermint, chamomile, and eyebright teas. Drinking these teas can improve lives by allowing individuals to read, watch TV, and perform daily activities without discomfort. Many people have found relief from dry eyes using herbal teas as a complement to traditional treatments.
